t_ch: 他们才不是什么怪物！
t_en: They were no monsters!

t_ch: 不是怪物，恩公，那是「{RUBY_B#「长生病」}魔阴身{RUBY_E#}」。
t_en: Those were not to be confused with monsters, benefactors. We call them {RUBY_B#Long-Life Curse}mara-struck{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 杨叔，这儿有个人受伤了……
t_en: Mr. Yang, someone's hurt...

t_ch: 大家小心，做好准备。
t_en: Get ready, and watch your backs.

t_ch: 包在本姑娘身上！
t_en: Leave it to me!

t_ch: 和离开时一样，几无变化……
t_en: Virtually nothing has changed since I left...

t_ch: …我记忆中的罗浮，除去黑暗，就只有这里了。
t_en: ...apart from darkness, this place is all that I remember of the Luofu.

t_ch: 发不出去……
t_en: Can't send the message out...

t_ch: 网络受限，有些功能却是正常的？
t_en: The network is down, but some functions still work?

t_ch: 我在前面开路，辛苦二位也留神四周啦。
t_en: I'll lead the way, you two keep an eye on our surroundings.

t_ch: 在下只是一介行商……
t_en: Not exactly my expertise. I'm just a traveling merchant...

t_ch: 事关生命耶！你这人长得也算一表人才，怎么性格这么懒啊！
t_en: This is life and death, okay!? You look like you can handle yourself, how come you're so lazy?

t_ch: 闷葫芦，你怎么不吭声啊？发现危险就大声叫唤喔，可别仗着自己懂两下子，就逞血气之勇单打独斗啊！
t_en: Hey, silent but deadly, shout if you see any danger, okay? Don't think just because you know a move or two you can charge off by yourself!

t_ch: 长相和性格，也没有必然联系吧。
t_en: Bold of you to assume my appearance betrays my nature.

t_ch: 对了，那个…罗刹。你的大箱子里面是很重要的东西吗？咱们最好轻装上阵，非贵重物就别带啦，会拖累你的。
t_en: Hey, Luo... Luocha, what's in the box? Is it important? If not, you should leave it behind, we need to travel light.

t_ch: 这不是箱子，是一具棺柩；重要与否我不好说，但总不能将往生者弃在半路吧。
t_en: This isn't any old box, miss — it's a coffin. I can't speak to its importance, but leaving the dead behind doesn't sound very appropriate.

t_ch: 你们仙舟有习俗叫：落叶归根，不是么？
t_en: I remember that the Xianzhou has a custom of burying its dead close to home, no?

t_ch: …你为什么——呃，我不问了。
t_en: ...What's that gotta... Ugh, I won't ask.

t_ch: …别管…我，前面……
t_en: ...Up ahead... Please, leave me...

t_ch: 怎么突然停下了？
t_en: Why have we stopped?

t_ch: 小事小事，是要请各位恩公帮个小忙。
t_en: Don't worry, Miss. I have a favor to ask, that's all.

t_ch: 什么…小忙？我不干啊，要是一个不小心，你又要差云骑大哥们来押我们了。
t_en: Favor...? Count me out. What if we do it wrong and you get your cloud cronies to throw us in jail.

t_ch: 哎呀～方才是我失言，恩公不要往心里去啦。你瞧，前面的路都给堵住了。
t_en: *sigh* I shouldn't have doubted you, please accept my apologies. Our road ahead is barricaded, do you see?

t_ch: 想必是驻守的士卒动了那些集装箱，将它们当作路障。
t_en: The soldiers stationed here must have moved these into a roadblock.

t_ch: 咱们一起推开它？
t_en: Let's push them aside together?

t_ch: 恩公说笑了，这么大的货箱…就算是所有人一齐上都拿不下呢。
t_en: I'd like to see us try, haha... they're extremely heavy. Even with all of us, we wouldn't be able to move one.

t_ch: 倒不是贬低各位的本事。恩公若是一不留神闪了腰，我心里也过意不去呀。
t_en: Not that I'm doubting your skill, but I'd feel terrible if you hurt yourself in the process.

t_ch: 只见杨叔挥挥手…
t_en: I'm sure Mr. Yang could clear them out...

t_ch: 杨叔可不是你的起重机啦！
t_en: What? Does he look like a crane to you?

t_ch: 恩公说笑了，这么大的货箱…就算是所有人一齐上都拿不下呢。
t_en: I'd like to see us try, haha... they're extremely heavy. Even with all of us, we wouldn't be able to move one.

t_ch: 倒不是贬低各位的本事。恩公若是一不留神闪了腰，我心里也过意不去呀。
t_en: Not that I'm doubting your skill, but I'd feel terrible if you hurt yourself in the process.

t_ch: 小女子没记错的话，积玉坊的货箱是通过操作台周转运送的。
t_en: If I'm not mistaken, the containers in the Trove of Verdure can be moved around using a control panel.

t_ch: 恩公身手矫健，只要找到操作台，然后操作一下，我们就能继续前进了。怎么样，很简单吧？
t_en: You've already put on quite the show — do you think you'll be able to find and operate the control panel? Should be simple, right?

t_ch: 很简单！
t_en: Right!

t_ch: 我就知道，这样的小困难根本难不住你。
t_en: I knew it. This will be a breeze for you.

t_ch: 很简单吧？那你怎么不做。
t_en: Right! So why don't you do it?

t_ch: 小女子身体孱弱，实在无能为力……
t_en: Oh, I'm afraid I'm not built for hard labor...

t_ch: 她真的好会差遣人喔。
t_en: She sure knows how to boss people around.

t_ch: 只消穿过这里，搭上去星槎海的舶船，到了安全地带就用不着再东躲西藏啦。
t_en: We just need to pass through here, take the barge to Starskiff Haven, and we'll be safe.

t_ch: 奇怪，这儿的门本该是打开的呀？
t_en: Strange, the gate here is supposed to be open.

t_ch: 又有人把路给堵上了？
t_en: Someone blocked our way again?

t_ch: 真是毫无悬念啊。
t_en: *sigh* I'm getting used to it.

t_ch: 前面的区域以后再来探索吧？
t_en: How about we explore the area ahead of us later?

t_ch: 咦，现在可不是回头或绕路的时候。
t_en: Huh? Now's not the time to turn back or take a detour.

t_ch: 肯定是有人动了手脚，待我试试把锁撬开……
t_en: Seems like someone wants to make things difficult for us. Let me see if I can pry it open...

t_ch: 还是让{NICKNAME}来吧。球棒虽然比不上物理学圣剑，总比手指好用。
t_en: Let {NICKNAME} do it. A bat's not as good as a crowbar, but it's definitely better than your fingers.

t_ch: 这下我成开锁工具了吗？
t_en: Am I a lock picker now?

t_ch: 你还想真让这位狐狸小姐动手？就她那懒惰性子，多少年才能打开这扇门啊。
t_en: What, you'd rather leave it to our fox lady here? She'll probably break a nail, and then we'll never hear the end of it...

t_ch: 我的头脑足以打开这把锁！
t_en: Prepare to meet your maker, lock!

t_ch: 好耶好耶，全看你的啦。
t_en: Yeah, yeah, we're watching.

t_ch: 不是搬箱就是开锁…好像咱们才是本地人，是给外来游客停云小姐引路的。
t_en: Moving containers, picking locks... Seems like we're doing all the heavy-lifting just so Miss Tingyun can enjoy the scenery.

t_ch: 呵呵，恩公又说笑话～
t_en: Hehe, am I really such a nuisance~?

t_ch: 一路过来可真不容易，尾巴毛都蹭掉了不少。唉，我的漂亮尾巴……
t_en: *sigh* Finally. Look how many tail hairs I lost on the way. Oh, my beautiful tail...

t_ch: 好在这一路上虽意外连连，最后一节却没出岔子。恩公们瞧，这就是小女子说的舶船了。
t_en: We had a difficult start, but things got easier. Look, that's the barge I was talking about.

t_ch: 咱们谁有…驾照吗？
t_en: Anyone... got a license for this thing?

t_ch: 恩公不必担忧。身为接渡使，星槎操舵可是咱们这行的必备技能。
t_en: Don't fret. All Amicassadors know their way around a starskiff.

t_ch: 我本来是不担忧，但她一副胸有成竹的样子，我就担心起来了……
t_en: I was perfectly calm until she volunteered to help...

t_ch: 这船…有自动档吗？
t_en: Is this... manual or automatic?

t_ch: 恩公不必担忧。身为接渡使，星槎操舵可是咱们这行的必备技能。
t_en: Don't fret. All Amicassadors know their way around a starskiff.

t_ch: 我本来是不担忧，但她一副胸有成竹的样子，我就担心起来了……
t_en: I was perfectly calm until she volunteered to help...

t_ch: 好！这船交给我了！
t_en: Nice! I'm driving!

t_ch: 用不着恩公操劳。身为接渡使，星槎操舵可是咱们这行的必备技能。
t_en: Uh-uh. Just sit back, and relax. All Amicassadors know their way around a starskiff.

t_ch: 我本来是不担忧，但她一副胸有成竹的样子，我就担心起来了……
t_en: I was perfectly calm until she volunteered to help...

t_ch: 还请各位船上稍坐。我们这就出发。
t_en: Please take your seats, everyone. We'll be off right away.

t_ch: 不对啊，这儿应该有条通道的…怎么不见啦？
t_en: There's supposed to be a passage... Why can't I see it?

t_ch: 有人动了流云渡的操纵台，用货箱堵住了路。
t_en: Somebody used the Cloudford control panel to block the road with containers.

t_ch: 可恶，谁干的好事啊！怎么办，本姑娘对机巧玩意儿向来一窍不通…等会儿，你为什么会知道？！
t_en: Ughh, who did this? I'm terrible with ingenium creations and stuff... Hold on, how do you know!?

t_ch: …都说了我刚从「曜青」仙舟调过来嘛，确实没怎么来过这儿。
t_en: ...I told you, I was only just transferred here from the Yaoqing. I don't come here very often.

t_ch: 谢天谢地，这里有艘星槎。
t_en: Phew, thank goodness, there's still one starskiff left.

t_ch: 看码头的线路，终点是星槎海的中枢。嗯，到那儿我们就安全啦。
t_en: Judging by where it's docked, it must be heading for Central Starskiff Haven. Nice, we'll be safe there.

t_ch: 走，咱们上船！
t_en: Come on, let's climb aboard!

t_ch: （必须尽快追上{NICKNAME}和三月……）
t_en: (I need to catch up with {NICKNAME} and March as fast as possible...)

t_ch: 不要到处乱跑，眼下这情况，咱们几个一起走才比较安全。
t_en: Don't go running off anywhere — we'll be safer if we stick together.

t_ch: 这船是通往哪里的？
t_en: Where does it go?

t_ch: 离开流云渡吗？
t_en: Leave Cloudford now?

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 稍等。
t_en: Not yet.

t_ch: 我是行商啊，熟悉仙舟有何奇怪。倒是你一个云骑，好像全不熟悉本地的路线似的。
t_en: I'm a merchant, is it really that strange that I know my way around the Xianzhou? Now, for a Cloud Knight to be unfamiliar with the local topography...

t_ch: （现在可不是分心干别的事的时候。）
t_en: (Now it's not the time to get distracted.)

t_ch: 闷葫芦先生，你这是要跑哪儿去啊？
t_en: Hey, silent but deadly, where are you going?

t_ch: 各位止步。前方正在执行排查维护工作，需得确认安全才能再度开放。
t_en: Go no further. The area ahead is currently under inspection. The lockdown will be lifted when the safety is ensured.

t_ch: 一页信纸静静躺在观景客厢的桌上，从质地与样式看来是仙舟所出。
t_en: A letter quietly lies on the table in the passenger car. Judging from its texture and style, it's from the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 纸面上，寄信人与收信者的名字全都付之阙如，只有一丝黯淡墨迹隐隐闪过。
t_en: On the paper, the names of the sender and recipient are all omitted, with only a faint trace of dim ink vaguely visible.

t_ch: 打开看看。
t_en: Open it.

t_ch: 你展开信纸，墨迹随即浮现，仿佛有看不见的手执笔如剑，利落挥洒。片刻后，纸上的内容恢复如初。
t_en: You unfold the letter, and the ink immediately becomes visible. It's as if an invisible hand holds the pen like a sword, making swift, decisive strokes. After a moment, the content regains its original appearance.

t_ch: 算了吧。
t_en: Forget it.

t_ch: 这是…一封信？
t_en: Is this... a letter?

t_ch: （倒悬古海，重游故地…这是封寄给丹恒的信。是什么人送来的？问问列车长吧……）
t_en: (Upend the ancient sea and return to old ground... It's a letter for Dan Heng, but who is it from? I should ask the conductor...)

t_ch: 帕！帕！…嗯？{NICKNAME}乘客，有事吗？
t_en: Heave-ho! Heave-ho! ...Huh? Trailblazer, do you need help with something?

t_ch: 桌上有一封信…
t_en: There's a letter on the table...

t_ch: 这信是谁送来的？
t_en: Who sent the letter?

t_ch: 帕姆一直在这儿打扫，没看见有什么信呀！
t_en: Pom-Pom's been cleaning up this whole time and didn't see any letters!

t_ch: 真是见鬼了…
t_en: This is creepy...

t_ch: 难道有人偷偷上了列车？
t_en: Did someone sneak onto the Express?

t_ch: 不、不要吓唬帕姆！为什么老是有怪东西闯进列车里？帕、帕姆要提高列车的安保等级帕！
t_en: Wh—What!? Don't scare Pom-Pom like that! Why are there always strange things sneaking onto our train? P—Pom-Pom is going to upgrade the security level around here!

t_ch: 这信上写了什么？
t_en: What does the letter say?

t_ch: （将信笺给帕姆看）
t_en: (Hand the letter over to Pom-Pom)

t_ch: 看来是一封邀请函…会是谁寄出的呢？丹恒乘客在罗浮仙舟上还有朋友吗？没记错的话，他说过自己被永久放逐了帕？
t_en: It looks like an invitation... Hmm... Who could have sent it? Does Dan Heng have any friends on the Luofu? If Pom-Pom remembers correctly, he said he was exiled from there, right?

t_ch: 朋友…景元？
t_en: Friends... Jing Yuan...?

t_ch: （不可能。如果是将军送来的信，为何会没有落款？）
t_en: (Unlikely. If the general sent the letter, he would've signed it.)

t_ch: 也可能是敌人…刃？
t_en: Enemies... Blade...?

t_ch: （不会吧。丹恒的敌人，会发出这样的邀请吗？）
t_en: (I highly doubt Dan Heng's enemy would send an invitation letter...)

t_ch: 不如把信给丹恒乘客瞧瞧吧？眼下他应该待在{RUBY_B#资料室}老地方{RUBY_E#}帕。
t_en: Why don't you show Dan Heng the letter? You know {RUBY_B#Archives}where{RUBY_E#} to find him.

t_ch: 没人来过这节车厢，究竟是谁把信送进来的？
t_en: I'm certain that no one entered this car — so who delivered the letter?

t_ch: …害帕！
t_en: ...This is giving me the shivers!

t_ch: 怎么了？{NICKNAME}，找我有事吗？
t_en: What is it, {NICKNAME}? Do you need something?

t_ch: 关于那封匿名信…
t_en: About the anonymous letter...

t_ch: 哦…你想听听后来发生的事情？
t_en: Oh... are you interested in what happened?

t_ch: 这封信的出现令人感到不安。你唤起云吟，一团水珠自空中凝成，浸入信纸。
t_en: The appearance of this letter makes you feel uneasy. You evoke the cloudhymn, and a droplet of water condenses from the air and soaks into the letter.

t_ch: 原本的字迹隐没不见了，而另一行笔墨随着水痕洇开——
t_en: The handwriting disappears, and another line of writing blurs due to the water stain —

t_ch: …果然没那么简单，让{NICKNAME}先行离开是明智之举，不能再因这件事让他们涉险了。
t_en: Just as I thought, there was more than met the eye... Letting {NICKNAME} leave first was a wise choice — I can't put them in danger again because of this.

t_ch: 可如果这不是{RUBY_B#刃}那家伙{RUBY_E#}寄来的信，又会是谁呢？
t_en: But if {RUBY_B#Blade}he{RUBY_E#} didn't send the letter... then who did?

t_ch: 也许我该走一趟<color=#dbc291ff>神策府</color>，向景元打听下星核猎手的动向……
t_en: Maybe I should make a trip to <color=#dbc291ff>the Seat of Divine Foresight</color>. I need to ask Jing Yuan about the Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 「人有五名，代价有三」。
t_en: Of five people, three must pay a price.

t_ch: 阔别许久回到这里…
t_en: A long time has passed since I visited this place...

t_ch: 此处毫无变化，一如数百年前。
t_en: Everything looks the same... just as it was hundreds of years ago.

t_ch: 她到底要待到什么时候…
t_en: How long does she plan on staying?

t_ch: 这不是丹恒先生吗？
t_en: Well, if it isn't Dan Heng.

t_ch: 打扰了，二位。
t_en: Greetings.

t_ch: 我有事求见将军。
t_en: I need to speak to the general about something.

t_ch: 打扰了，我有事求见将军。
t_en: Greetings. I need to speak to the general about something.

t_ch: 若是为了彦卿在追捕时贸然动手一事，前来检定伤情、索要赔偿…彦卿认罚。我未来百年的薪饷尽可拿来作赔偿。
t_en: Hmph, looking for compensation for the injuries I gave you? I accept full responsibility — they can deduct my next hundred years' salary if they like...

t_ch: 不必了。
t_en: That won't be necessary.

t_ch: 不必了，我并非为此而来。云骑行使职责，并无过错。我当时一意突围，也多有得罪了。
t_en: That won't be necessary, the Cloud Knights were doing their duty. I should be apologizing for any damage I caused myself.

t_ch: 有其他事…
t_en: I'm here about something else...

t_ch: 云骑行使职责，并无过错。我求见将军，是想打听星核猎手的动向。
t_en: The Cloud Knights were doing their duty. I'm here to ask the general about the Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 您来得不是时候，将军有要务在身，今天怕是见不着了。但他临行前留下了口信…丹恒先生，你可认得陛阶上的那人？
t_en: You came at a bad time. The General is busy with urgent work — you probably won't be able to meet with him today... but he did leave a message. Dan Heng, do you recognize the person on the steps?

t_ch: 似乎认得。
t_en: I think so.

t_ch: 说不上认得，只是有些面熟，是将军的客人？
t_en: I can't say that I know her, but she does look a little familiar. Is she a guest of the general's?

t_ch: 不认得…
t_en: No...

t_ch: …不认识。既然将军不在，那我改日再来。
t_en: ...No. If the general isn't available, I'll come back another day.

t_ch: 哦，您记不得她了？这样啊…持明转世，前生的一切果真烟消云散了。
t_en: ...You don't remember her? I see, I suppose the memories of your previous life really were wiped clean after the rebirth.

t_ch: 这位是罗浮仙舟的前代剑首「镜流」大人，与你的前世之身「饮月君」可是生死之交。不仅如此，她还是景元将军的…恩师。
t_en: That is Jingliu, the previous Sword Champion of the Xianzhou Luofu. She was the closest friend of Imbibitor Lunae, your previous incarnation. She was also the general's... master.

t_ch: 据战事文牍记载，倒在她剑下的丰饶之民数不胜数。造翼者的羽卫，步离人的父狼，连高如山岳的器兽也当不住她的一击，可谓是名噪一时的传奇。
t_en: According to records of wars past, her blade has slain countless Denizens of Abundance. Not even the Feather Guards of the Wingweaver, the Father Wolf of the borisin, or the gigantic Mechabeast could withstand a single strike from her. She's a renowned legend.

t_ch: 但那是很久之前的过去了。可惜，可惜，虽英雄如此，却也无法解脱魔阴。据说镜流大人最终神智狂乱、大开杀戒，成了逃亡域外的重犯。
t_en: However, those are ancient stories. It's a shame that even a hero like her wasn't able to break free of mara. It is said that in the end, Jingliu lost her sanity and slaughtered anyone in sight. She was deemed a criminal and fled to outer worlds.

t_ch: 以她的能耐，本无人能将其捉拿归案。但不知为何，她竟与某位伪装成行商的嫌犯一同来到罗浮，并宣称要自首伏罪……
t_en: With her powers, you'd think there would be no one capable of bringing her in. For some reason, she arrived on the Luofu with a suspect disguised as a traveling merchant and said she wanted to turn herself in...

t_ch: 条件是，在受审前她要有一日自由，前往鳞渊境与老朋友们再会一面——而更离谱的是，景元居然答应了！
t_en: Her surrender came with one condition — that she be allowed a day of freedom before the trial. She wanted to travel to Scalegorge Waterscape and meet with her old friends one last time... I can't believe Jing Yuan actually agreed!

t_ch: 他临行前交托我们的任务，便是陪同镜流，度过她在罗浮上的最后一日。你明白了吧，这其实不是「接待贵客」，而是「押送囚犯」——
t_en: Before he left, he ordered us to accompany Jingliu during her last day on the Luofu. We're hosting an honored guest, we're escorting a criminal...

t_ch: 青镞突然顿住语声，和她一样，你察觉到周遭的空气冷了下来。
t_en: Qingzu suddenly stops talking. Like her, you perceive a chill settling in the air around you.

t_ch: 饮月，你来啦。
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae, you're here.

t_ch: 既然来了，何不上前叙叙旧？
t_en: Since you've traveled all this way — why don't you come and talk with me?

t_ch: 还是我该称呼你今生的名字，丹恒？
t_en: Or should I call you by your name in this lifetime — "Dan Heng?"

t_ch: 去吧，丹恒先生。她点名要你上前问话呢。
t_en: Master Dan Heng, she's asking for you specifically.

t_ch: 丹恒先生小心为妙，这位大姐姐可难缠的很……
t_en: Be careful. Don't underestimate her...

t_ch: 咱们可以启程了。唔…我打算重游几处故地，酹酒一杯，缅怀旧事。
t_en: We can head out now. Hmm... I would like to visit a few places from my memories, enjoy a glass of wine, and reminisce about the good times.

t_ch: 你以为自己的身份是观光客么？
t_en: Have you mistaken yourself for a tourist?

t_ch: 我可以是。
t_en: I would make a wonderful tourist.

t_ch: 小弟弟，不要愁眉苦脸的。我已向联盟自首，便不会背弃诺言，一走了之。何况你家将军也答应了我的请求。
t_en: Lower your guard, young man. I have already surrendered myself to the Alliance, and I will keep my word. Besides, your general has already agreed to my request.

t_ch: 而饮月，你要和我同去，不可以拒绝。无论你对自己的前世是否在意，收下我的信，便是答应了我的邀约。
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae, you must accompany me — I won't take no for an answer. Regardless of whether you're still interested in your previous life, accepting my letter means that you've accepted my invitation.

t_ch: …带路吧。
t_en: Lead the way...

t_ch: 从哪儿开始呢？是先去「迴星港」？还是「工造司」？
t_en: Where should we start? Should we go to Stargazer Navalia first? Or the Artisanship Commission?

t_ch: 有好多回忆啊…可它们在我脑袋里转呀转，怎么也抓不住……
t_en: So many memories are swirling inside my head... I can't get a grasp on any of them...

t_ch: 唉…仙舟人寿至千岁后，每活一日便像是背负着山峰，辗转于迷宫。
t_en: ...When the people of the Xianzhou live to be more than a thousand years old, each day is like carrying the weight of a mountain through an interminable maze.

t_ch: 选个地方而已，有那么难吗？我替你选吧，就去迴星港。
t_en: How hard is it to choose a destination? We're going to Stargazer Navalia — problem solved.

t_ch: 哈哈，小孩子就是有活力呢。就依你说的办。
t_en: Haha, the young are the antidote. Stargazer Navalia it is.

t_ch: 若是要看星槎，在玉界门边上抬头就有的是。为什么要大老远跑这儿来？
t_en: If it's starskiffs you wanted to see, we could've headed to the Jade Gate. Why did we come all the way here?

t_ch: 小弟弟，你可听过狐人飞行士「白珩」的名字？
t_en: Young man, have you ever heard of Baiheng, the foxian pilot?

t_ch: 白珩…白珩…这个名字，似乎在哪里见过……
t_en: Baiheng... I think I've seen that name somewhere before...

t_ch: 白珩…
t_en: Baiheng...

t_ch: 这名字对你来说陌生得像是一阵偶然掠过的轻风，唤不起任何情绪。但不知为何，耳畔却传来一阵声音。
t_en: This name is unfamiliar to you and elicits no feelings. However, a voice inexplicably rings in your ears.

t_ch: 「让高贵的龙尊行云布雨，把敌阵淹没就好了。咱们这些陪衬只要在天上看着就行了对吧？」
t_en: Let the noble high elder drown the enemies in rain — we just need to watch from the sky, I presume?

t_ch: 飞行士…
t_en: Pilot...

t_ch: 关于飞行，你什么也记不起来。尊贵如你的前生，本无需亲手操舵。而今世身为无名客的你也未曾接触星槎。
t_en: The concept of flight holds no memories for you. In your previous life of nobility, you were spared the task of taking the helm. As an unnamed traveler in this life, you have not yet come into contact with a starskiff.

t_ch: 但不知为何，耳畔却传来一阵声音，在呼啸的风声中惊慌失形。
t_en: Yet, there's a voice, inexplicably echoing in your ears, becoming agitated in the midst of the howling wind.

t_ch: 「星槎撑不住啦！龙尊大人，你那么能耐，就快些在它散架前接住咱们啊啊啊啊——」
t_en: The starskiff won't hold much longer! High Elder, catch us before it falls apart!

t_ch: 饮月，若是想起了什么，不妨说来听听。
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae, if memories are returning to you, share them.

t_ch: 我…什么都想不起来。
t_en: I... can't remember anything.

t_ch: 我什么都记不得了。
t_en: I can't remember anything.

t_ch: 说谎。你也学坏了。
t_en: But you've learned how to lie.

t_ch: 我只记得一些碎片…
t_en: I only remember fragments...

t_ch: 她…似乎是「饮月君」的战友。
t_en: I think... she was one of Imbibitor Lunae's comrades.

t_ch: 是战友，也是朋友。是饮月的，也是我们的。
t_en: She was a comrade and friend both to Imbibitor Lunae and all of us.

t_ch: 也罢…都是些过往云烟。之所以来「迴星港」，只是想在此祭拜她。
t_en: Well... that's all in the past now. The reason we came to Stargazer Navalia is because I wanted to pay tribute to her here.

t_ch: 哦…是狐人的「慰灵奠仪」?
t_en: Huh...? You mean the foxian soul-soothing ceremony?

t_ch: 嗯，将代表逝者的星槎送入星空。我当时走得匆忙，没能与她告别，至今仍有遗憾。在离开罗浮前，想了却这桩心事。
t_en: Yes... the sending off of a starskiff. It represents the voyage of the deceased into the stars. Due to circumstances beyond my control, I never had the chance to bid farewell to her... It is something I have regretted to this day.

t_ch: 不过关于星槎制造，我确是一窍不通，只知道这儿能造出星槎。
t_en: As for procuring a starskiff, I know they can be manufactured here... but I know nothing of the process.

t_ch: 我明白了…跟我来吧。
t_en: I see... Follow me.

t_ch: 星核邪祟尚未除尽，当心。
t_en: Careful. There are still Stellaron spirits lurking around.

t_ch: 不必担心我们二人。
t_en: No need to worry about the two of us.

t_ch: 虽然我也不懂星槎制造…
t_en: I don't know anything about manufacturing starskiffs either...

t_ch: 但让这些机枢动起来倒也不复杂。
t_en: ...but getting these machines to work shouldn't be too hard.

t_ch: 咱们到了。迴星港中的船舶但凡生产，起始处就在这里。
t_en: We've arrived. All ship production in Stargazer Navalia starts here.

t_ch: 只消对着那些机关输入指令，培育星槎种子的器皿就会动起来，要不了多久，空港里就会多一艘船。
t_en: Just enter the command into the device. The vessel used to cultivate starskiff seeds will be activated, and a ship will appear in the skyport soon after.

t_ch: …啊，我想起来了，「白珩」这个名字，我在书中读到过。
t_en: Ah... I remember now. I've seen Baiheng's name in books before.

t_ch: 你爱读书？看不出来啊。
t_en: You like to read? I couldn't tell.

t_ch: 干什么！虽然我平日读书不多，但受训战略时可是被将军逼着读了好些馆藏古籍。记得其中有一册名叫《涯海星槎胜览》，作者就是这个名儿。
t_en: Hey! I don't read much, but the General forced me to finish a bunch of ancient volumes during my training. I remember one called Views of the Universe From a Starskiff — the author's name was "Baiheng."

t_ch: 不错，白珩就是写下那本游记的人。
t_en: That's right, Baiheng was the one who wrote that travelogue.

t_ch: 那书行笔风趣，十页有九是作者在不同世界里星槎坠毁，频频遇险的经历，还夹杂着对当地物种和生态的记录。
t_en: It was actually really interesting — <unbreak>90%</unbreak> of it was about starskiff crashes on different worlds and dangerous situations. It also had records of local species and ecosystems.

t_ch: 我当时便想，动不动坠毁星槎…这样的人也能算是飞行士么？可转念又想，每次她都能化险为夷，安然生还，这份运气真是令人惊叹。
t_en: I remember thinking — how could an ace pilot crash-land her starskiff so many times... then I realized that every time, she made the best of the situation and got back alive. Her luck really was out of this world.

t_ch: 是啊，她的运气从来都坏得惊人。但凡驾驶星槎出征，不是阴差阳错被丰饶民的巨兽当点心吞下，便是在敌人的大后方坠机。
t_en: Indeed, her luck always astonished us. Whenever she piloted a mission, you could almost guarantee a narrow escape from the jaws of some Abundance behemoth, or a miraculous return from behind enemy lines.

t_ch: 经她之手的星槎没几艘能原样回港，天舶司的人背地里都称她是「星槎杀手」。
t_en: Very few starskiffs she piloted were able to return to port in one piece. The folks at the Sky-Faring Commission secretly referred to her as the "Starskiff Killer."

t_ch: 她那张乌鸦嘴也是，连蒙带猜说出口的坏事，十有八九都要应验成真。一来二去，云骑里敢和她同行的人也不剩几个了。
t_en: She also possessed an uncanny ability to predict future events... inauspicious ones, at least. Every ill omen that came out of her mouth would sure enough come true. Very few Cloud Knights had the courage to accompany her on missions.

t_ch: 可唯独在活命这件事上…她的运气却又好得惊人。无论怎样的艰险，她总能逢凶化吉。
t_en: However, when it came to surviving... her fortunes were incredible. Even in the worst situations imaginable, she was always able to turn the tide.

t_ch: 运气也是实力的一种…希望这艘专为「星槎杀手」造的船，也能像她一样回归星空。
t_en: Luck is a type of strength, after all... I hope that this ship, built for the Starskiff Killer, will also be able to return to the stars.

t_ch: 这样就好，星槎的流水线动起来了。
t_en: That'll do it. The starskiff assembly line has been activated.

t_ch: 要不了多久，星槎就会自塑成形，驶入空港。咱们去那儿等着吧。
t_en: The starskiff will take shape and enter the skyport. Let's go wait for it there, it won't be long.

t_ch: 有劳啦，小弟弟。
t_en: Thank you, young man.

t_ch: 我带来了你的酒壶…白珩。这原本是那人为你雕琢的赠物，可他没能亲手送出。
t_en: Baiheng, I've brought your flagon... This was a gift handmade by him, though he never got the chance to give it to you in person.

t_ch: 对不起，直到最近，我才找回了它。也只有把它送回你身边，我的梦魇才能平息片刻。
t_en: I'm sorry it took so long for me to find it. Only by bringing it to you will my nightmares be calmed.

t_ch: 而你要我做的，我一定会做到，哪怕为此要斩落天上的星星，我也绝不毁诺。
t_en: I will fulfill what you asked of me. I will keep my word, even if it requires me to cut down the stars in the sky.

t_ch: 走吧。接下来，我们去工造司。
t_en: Let's go. The Artisanship Commission is next.

t_ch: 唉，建木复生，孽物遍地。连工造司的机要之物「造化洪炉」都快保不住了。
t_en: ... Since the Arbor's resurrection, the land has been crawling with abominations. *sniff* Even the Artisanship Commission's prized Creation Furnace is hanging on by a thread.

t_ch: 事发突然，听说许多匠人学徒仓促逃命，只剩一位老师傅坚守在此，等来了外援，才救下这造化洪炉。
t_en: The crisis happened too suddenly — all the craftsmen and apprentices fled. Only a single master craftsman was left to hold the line, until reinforcements arrived. That's how the Creation Furnace was saved.

t_ch: 有意思。轻忽性命，顽固不化，倒是让我想起一位眼高于顶的故人。他要是目睹工造司这片狼藉，怕是会大笑仙舟人无能。
t_en: Interesting. Stubborn beyond valuing his own life... That reminds me of an extremely arrogant acquaintance. If he were to see the chaos of the Artisanship Commission today, he would burst into laughter at the Xianzhou's incompetence.

t_ch: 什么话！面对险境，珍惜生命等待云骑又有什么不对？
t_en: What's that supposed to mean!? What's wrong with saving your own life and waiting for the Cloud Knights to arrive?

t_ch: 既然如此，危难时刻云骑又在哪里？我听说拯救此处的可是一行化外旅客。
t_en: And yet where were the Cloud Knights when danger was at the door? From what I heard, it was a group of outworlders that came to the rescue.

t_ch: 罗浮洞天广大…再加上不少部队出发配合曜青征伐，云骑也是首尾难顾，哪来那么多人手到处驻扎来着？
t_en: The Luofu's delves are big places... Besides, some of the troops were deployed to the Yaoqing's campaign. The Cloud Knights were spread thin — it was impossible to guard everywhere at once.

t_ch: 小弟弟，不如再来一次上回的比试如何？让我瞧瞧你引以为傲的剑术又有几分进展。
t_en: Hmph. Young man, why don't we continue our previous contest? Show me whether the sword techniques you are so proud of have improved.

t_ch: 你只是想借我的手把这儿打扫打扫吧？一个自首的犯人真的可以提这么多要求吗？
t_en: You just want me to do all the work. You think I'm gonna grant endless requests for a criminal?

t_ch: 这次由你先行，我们在造化洪炉处碰头。
t_en: You go on ahead this time. We'll meet up at the Creation Furnace.

t_ch: 你特意支走那个孩子，有何目的？
t_en: ...Why did you send him away?

t_ch: 不为别的，你我也许久没有比试过了。
t_en: It is of no importance... but you and I haven't had a decent contest in ages.

t_ch: 我不打算动手。
t_en: I don't plan on fighting.

t_ch: 我也没要求你把枪指向我，这儿的孽物不够你出手吗？
t_en: I'm not asking you to point your spear at me. Could it be that the abominations here aren't enough to motivate you?

t_ch: 别走，还未决出胜负呢。
t_en: Leaving so soon? We haven't settled this yet.

t_ch: 即便是转世之身，你的一招一式却与那人并无不同。
t_en: Even after your rebirth, your moves and techniques haven't changed one bit.

t_ch: 我施展这些动作，就像…
t_en: When I move... it's like...

t_ch: …就像你从未忘却。
t_en: ...like you never forgot.

t_ch: 加把劲啊，大姐姐！
t_en: Come on, already!

t_ch: 这回你怎么这么慢啊？
t_en: What took you so long this time?

t_ch: 不必惶急，饮月，我们再走一程。
t_en: Don't get anxious, Imbibitor Lunae. We have all the time in the world.

t_ch: 这杆枪，依旧认得你这个主人。
t_en: This spear still recognizes you as its master.

t_ch: 饮月，还记得为你打造它的人吗？
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae, do you know who crafted it for you?

t_ch: 从我被放逐起，它就跟在我的身边…
t_en: It's been with me ever since my exile...

t_ch: 我不记得自己是从什么时候开始挥舞它的。
t_en: I don't remember when I started wielding it.

t_ch: 你可以一次次说服自己是丹枫的转世，与他所犯下的罪责无关。你也可以坚持自己已经遗忘了一切。
t_en: You can continue to convince yourself that you are merely the reincarnation of Dan Feng and have nothing to do with his sins... and you can keep insisting that you have forgotten everything.

t_ch: 但你无法逃离战斗，丹恒，你的枪术与饮月所用的技艺如出一辙。
t_en: But you cannot run from combat, Dan Heng. Your spearmanship is identical to Imbibitor Lunae's.

t_ch: 「战斗就像一次次锻冶，用烈焰熔去杂质，展露一个人内在的本性。」
t_en: Combat is like smelting — purging the impurities with burning flames and exposing a person's true nature.

t_ch: 为你打造这杆「击云」枪的人曾这么对你说，还记得吗？
t_en: Those are the words of the one who made Cloud-Piercer for you. Do you remember?

t_ch: 我们几人中，要说谁和他走得最近，那只能是你。真是奇怪，眼高于顶的家伙竟会和另一个拿鼻孔瞧人的家伙相谈甚欢。
t_en: Out of all of us, you were the closest to him. It was strange, really — to see someone so arrogant get along with someone so proud.

t_ch: …应星？
t_en: ...Yingxing?

t_ch: 在梦中，你曾见到那个面目模糊的男人，他倨傲不恭地立在安放「击云」的枪架前。
t_en: In your dreams, you see a man with an indistinct face, standing arrogantly and irreverently in front of the spear rack where the "Cloud-Piercer" is placed.

t_ch: 「这杆枪，锐利得足可穿透龙鳞。小心，可别被它伤到了，龙尊大人。」
t_en: This spear is sharp enough to pierce dragon scales. Be careful, High Elder, lest you hurt yourself with it.

t_ch: …应星。
t_en: Yingxing...

t_ch: （努力回忆那人）
t_en: (Try hard to remember)

t_ch: 那个人的名字在你思绪的边缘如风中落叶般飞舞，却始终不能清晰可见。
t_en: The name of that person flutters at the edge of your thoughts like a leaf in the wind, but it remains elusive and unclear.

t_ch: 「宁如飞萤赴火，不作樗木长春。我会让所有仙舟人知道，应星刹那的一生比他们漫长无用的寿数更有价值。」
t_en: I'd rather leave this world in a blaze than live until the end of time. I will let all of the Xianzhou know that a brief moment of my life is worth more than their long and meaningless lives.

t_ch: 嗯，你还记得这个名字。
t_en: Mm... you still remember his name.

t_ch: 他还是个孩子的时候，便一心要对孽物复仇，远渡星海来仙舟求艺。
t_en: When he was but a child, he swore to take revenge on the abominations. That's what drove him to travel across the stars to the Xianzhou — to receive training.

t_ch: 初见他时，脑袋还高不过我的剑，却夸口要以百岁之身，学尽工造司万般匠艺。区区一只小狗，竟傲慢得和龙尊不相上下。
t_en: When I first met him, he wasn't even as tall as my sword... but he was boasting about mastering all the crafts of the Artisanship Commission by the time he was a hundred years old. Hehe, the little brat was no less arrogant than the high elder.

t_ch: 我本瞧不上他那狷狂的个性，不料再度相遇时，他所造兵器已令匠人师傅望尘莫及，就连颁授给工造司之首的「百冶」头衔也被他摘得。
t_en: At first, I wasn't too fond of his defiant nature, but when we met again, he was able to craft weapons that astonished the master craftsmen. He even snatched the Artisanship Commission title of "Furnace Master."

t_ch: 可惜联盟不会让一介短生种接掌工造司。到头来，他也只得在我们这些异类身旁寻求温暖。
t_en: Unfortunately, the Alliance would never allow a short-life species to run the Artisanship Commission. He was only able to prove his worth alongside us outcasts in the end.

t_ch: 好慢啊…
t_en: What's taking so long...?

t_ch: 她该不会想故意差走我吧…
t_en: Did she get rid of me on purpose...

t_ch: 这不是丹恒先生吗！真是稀客呀。该不是最近身体有什么贵恙？来来，这边请。
t_en: Dan Heng!? What a surprise! Are you not feeling well? Please, come this way.

t_ch: 我不是来看病的…
t_en: I'm not here for a check-up...

t_ch: 我身边这位想来问症。
t_en: She's here for a consultation.

t_ch: 白露小姐，来问诊的不是我，是我身边这位。
t_en: Miss Bailu, she's here for a consultation.

t_ch: 喔？瞧你的样子，是想医好眼睛，还是别的什么病？
t_en: Oh, I see. Are you here for your eyes? Or is it something else?

t_ch: 我双眼无碍，将它蒙上只是不愿睹物思旧，坠入心魔。我最近神思纷乱，时有夜梦惊悸，想请龙女大人瞧瞧，可有安神的法子。
t_en: My eyes are fine. I cover them to avoid seeing that which reminds me of the past and riles my inner demons. My thoughts have been disordered lately, and I experience occasional night terrors. Do you have a way to soothe my mind, Dragon Lady?

t_ch: 自诉病征听起来…倒不像是归我管的。
t_en: Those symptoms... don't sound like my area of expertise.

t_ch: 呸呸呸，是我多嘴啦，丹鼎司医士不挑病人。大姐姐，请您把手伸出来，咱们先从诊脉开始。待会再服下些透影虫，让我仔细瞧瞧。
t_en: Bah, who cares. The healers of the Alchemy Commission don't get to pick and choose their patients. Give me your hand — we'll start by checking your pulse. You'll need to take some translucence worms so I can take a closer look.

t_ch: 白露把脉片刻…
t_en: Bailu is taking someone's pulse...

t_ch: 好、好冷的手！
t_en: Y—Your hand... It's freezing!

t_ch: 你且等等。丹恒先生，这边说话。
t_en: Please wait. Dan Heng, can I speak to you for a minute?

t_ch: 你带来的这位朋友「病」的可真奇怪。
t_en: Your friend here has a very peculiar "condition."

t_ch: 她的脉象几近于无。按常理说来，这意味着她……
t_en: Her pulse is extremely faint... Normally, this would mean that she...

t_ch: 命不久矣。
t_en: She won't live much longer.

t_ch: …代表命不久矣。
t_en: ...She won't live much longer.

t_ch: 无药可医。
t_en: There's no cure.

t_ch: …代表无法医治。
t_en: ...There's no cure.

t_ch: 啊呸呸呸！请不要代替医士做出专业诊断。
t_en: Ah-ba-ba-ba... Let the healer make the professional diagnosis!

t_ch: 你的朋友情况却又不同，她表象体征栩栩如生，但抓住她的手诊脉时简直像是抓住了一块冰，丹腑和脉络间又像是暗河流转，搏动不休。
t_en: Your friend's situation is unique. She looks healthy, but her hands are cold as ice. It feels like there's a dark current running and pulsing between her core esse and her veins.

t_ch: 本小姐还真没遇到过这样的奇症，也许详加研究，可以录入医经。能带你朋友常来我这看诊吗？
t_en: I've never seen anything like it. With more research, I might be able to make a new entry in the medical records. Can you and your friend come back for follow-up visits?

t_ch: （委婉地拒绝）
t_en: (Decline respectfully)

t_ch: 今天过后，她就要离开罗浮了。
t_en: Today's her last day on the Luofu.

t_ch: 可惜，多留几日，我兴许有眉目能医好她。
t_en: What a shame. If she could stay for a few days, I might be able to figure out how to cure her.

t_ch: （直白地拒绝）
t_en: (Decline directly)

t_ch: 她是重犯，此事由云骑和十王司说了算。
t_en: She's a criminal. That's up to the Cloud Knights and the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 真是不可貌相，瞧她柔柔弱弱的样子，怎么会是…重犯？算了算了，不该本小姐知道的事情，不问便是。
t_en: Talk about not judging a book by its cover — she looks so kind and elegant... How could she be a criminal? Meh, not my concern.

t_ch: 唉，我尽力吧。接下来不管我要开什么药，她都得多喝热水才行。
t_en: ...I'll do my best. No matter what I administer, she's gonna need to drink a ton of warm water from this point on.

t_ch: 你跟我来。我开个还魂正气散的方子给她。用上这几味药材固然不能祛除乱象…等等，我那么大个药箱到哪儿去了？
t_en: Come with me, I'll prescribe her some soul-returning vitality infusion. It won't cure her, but it'll... Wait... where did my big medicine box go?

t_ch: 实在对不住，打从药王秘传被铲除后，司里的一切都乱了套。
t_en: I'm sorry, ever since the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus were expelled, things have been a mess around here.

t_ch: 能不能麻烦本事高强的丹恒先生跟我走一遭，去把药取来。
t_en: Dan Heng, you mind coming with me to retrieve my medicine? I could use your skills.

t_ch: 想来龙女大人是有麻烦了？我也同去。
t_en: Are you in trouble, Dragon Lady? I'll come along as well.

t_ch: 不不不，不麻烦了……
t_en: N—No, no... No trouble at all...

t_ch: 区区小事，不算麻烦。
t_en: It's no bother — a little walk will do me good.

t_ch: 哎…
t_en: Emm...

t_ch: 唔，药材的味道就在这附近了。
t_en: Hmm, I can smell the herbs. It's around here somewhere.

t_ch: 哪来的坏东西，把我药箱还来！
t_en: Hey! Give me back my medicine box!

t_ch: 气死我了！四条腿撒的飞快…别让它逃了！
t_en: Argh, that thing runs so fast! Don't let it get away!

t_ch: 呃欸欸欸！竟然还有个这么大的靠山！
t_en: Ahhhh! There's an even bigger monster protecting this one!

t_ch: 什么时候丹鼎司里连来看病的动物都这么多了？
t_en: When did so many creatures start visiting the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 小心。
t_en: Careful.

t_ch: 碍事的东西，我会为龙女顺手除去。我的剑寒凛冽，请站远些。
t_en: Allow me to eliminate these obstacles, Dragon Lady... Stand back, the chill of my sword freezes everything in its path.

t_ch: 我算是明白你的手为什么这么冷了。
t_en: So this is why your hands are so cold.

t_ch: 这份寒意，绝不是血肉之躯能扛下的…
t_en: This cold isn't something normal flesh could withstand...

t_ch: 好嘞，让我瞧瞧，安神草和壮气散都在。回去吧，可不能让病人久等。
t_en: Alright, let me make sure nothing's missing... Excellent, the calming-valerian and qi-strengthening infusions are here. Let's head back — never make a patient wait too long.

t_ch: 真是抱歉，让您久等啦。我这方剂虽然不能药到病除，但养护元气，稳定心神还是能办到的。
t_en: Apologies again for the delay. This prescription won't cure you, but it will nourish your body and calm your mind.

t_ch: 不必了，就算龙女大人的医术通神，对长生种的宿命恐怕也是无可奈何吧？
t_en: Very well. The fate of a long-life species is inevitable, even with the best doctors.

t_ch: 魔阴身？可我瞧你也不像啊？最近罗浮遇到了大麻烦，我为不少受魔阴身困扰的患者看过诊。但这些人不是语无伦次、神智失常，就是躯壳变异、样貌可怕。
t_en: You're... mara-struck? I don't see the symptoms in you at all. What with the incident on the Luofu, I've treated a number of mara-stricken patients recently. They all presented with signs of incoherence, delirium, or frightening physical mutations.

t_ch: 我看你身上也没长出什么奇形怪状的东西嘛。
t_en: You don't seem to have any of that...

t_ch: 我做了一笔交易，侥幸苟延残命。虽然眼下尚有思考的余力，但有一件事我却很清楚，我的心识已到了极限。
t_en: I made a deal that allowed me to retain what life I had left in me. I may be sane, but one thing is certain — my mind is already at its limit...

t_ch: 话虽如此…我还有许多未完成的夙愿，和尚未清偿的仇怨。
t_en: ...and yet I still have many unfulfilled wishes, and plenty of unfinished business.

t_ch: 喔？你很明白自己的症结所在啊。心病的事情，我也帮不上忙。不过医经上说，熄心则安身。如果能忘掉过去的事情，这病症还有挽回的余地。
t_en: I see — so, you've learned to live with your illness. I can't help much with issues of the spirit, but the medical records do mention that calming the mind is a way to heal the body. If you're able to forget about the past, there might be a chance for your symptoms to regress.

t_ch: 是吗？真遗憾。在所有的情绪中，仇恨最为强烈，我选择了它，握紧了它，用它来维持我的存在。
t_en: Really? How unfortunate... my hatred overshadows my other emotions. I chose to hold onto it, to embrace it, and to use it to maintain my existence.

t_ch: 我能握住的只剩下手中的剑和往日遗恨。如果连这些都放弃的话，我恐怕…会彻底堕入虚无。
t_en: All I have left are the sword in my hand and my hatred of the past. If I am without either of them... I would fall into an empty void.

t_ch: 谢谢你的建言，龙女大人，能见你一面，胜过药石百倍。
t_en: Thank you for your advice, Dragon Lady. Meeting you has provided more healing than any kind of medicine or remedy.

t_ch: 说起来，不知最近几日是否有缠着绷带的人前来求龙女大人看诊？
t_en: By the way, among those you've treated in recent days, did you notice a person in bandages?

t_ch: 缠着绷带？这么宽泛的特征，我可说不上来。仙舟上每天都有数不胜数的伤患来丹鼎司就医，建木灾变之后，受伤的人更是多了不少。
t_en: Bandages? That's a vague description, y'know... I'm not sure. We get injured patients from all over the Xianzhou for treatment, and the number of injuries we see has skyrocketed since the Ambrosial Arbor crisis.

t_ch: 我要问的那人身形高瘦，表情阴沉，一柄残破的剑从不离身。不知龙女大人有没有印象？
t_en: The person I'm inquiring about is tall, with a serious expression. He also carries a crude sword with him at all times. Have you seen someone like that, Dragon Lady?

t_ch: 这样的人啊……今天就有一个！
t_en: Hmm... Oh! Yes, someone came by today that matches that description!

t_ch: 他一定是受了剑伤，身上的血气一嗅便知。只是那伤似乎也没什么打紧，因为那人看起来神色如常，最后走时连我开的药也没拿。
t_en: He must've suffered some kind of sword wound — he had a strong scent of blood... though from the way he behaved, it must have been fairly minor. He didn't even take the medicine I prescribed.

t_ch: 呵，也许他想索求的是另一种药方吧。
t_en: Hm, he's probably looking for a different kind of medicine.

t_ch: 如此一来，该到的人便都到了。走吧，去会会我们的老朋友。
t_en: Well then, it would seem that everything is in place. Let's go and meet our old friend.

t_ch: 没有酒，只有苦涩，这样的聚会真是令人一言难尽啊。
t_en: A wineless gathering, accompanied by only sadness and bitterness. What a depressing reunion.

t_ch: 该是送我启程去虚陵的时候了，景元。
t_en: Jing Yuan, is it not time you sent me to the Xuling?

t_ch: 联盟法度，不容更改…可惜，你们的下一站并非虚陵，而是「玉阙」。
t_en: The laws of the Alliance cannot be subject to change... Unfortunately, your next stop will be the Yuque.

t_ch: 我将此事呈报元帅，怎料那位{RUBY_B#玉阙的爻光}「戎韬将军」{RUBY_E#}颇感兴趣，竟中道拦阻，设下「十方光映法界」，想先一步会会二位。
t_en: I reported everything to the Marshal, and {RUBY_B#Yao Guang of Yuque}the Seer Strategist{RUBY_E#} just so happened to take an interest in the case — they set up a Deca-Light Reflection Barrier in hopes of meeting the two of you beforehand.

t_ch: …景元，你还是老样子，总想挣扎着打破别人的布局。
t_en: Jing Yuan... You haven't changed at all — always trying to destroy the plans of others...

t_ch: 但是你我也好，云骑也罢，帝弓的将军们…都不过是神明弈局里的棋子，我已厌倦了走在被预设的命途之上。
t_en: ...but in the end, whether it be you, me, the Cloud Knights, or the generals of the Reignbow Arbiter... We are all just pawns in a game of the gods. I am sick and tired of treading on a predetermined path.

t_ch: 无妨，便陪你多走段路吧，但结局不会改变，我终会站在胜利的那一方。
t_en: No matter. I will deviate if you insist, but this does not change how things will end. Ultimately, I will stand on the side of victory.

t_ch: 那么这局对弈，我会奉陪到底。
t_en: Then I shall see this gamble through.

t_ch: 饮月，你接下来有什么打算？
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae, what do you plan on doing next?

t_ch: 我打算回到列车。
t_en: I'm going to return to the Express.

t_ch: 这场聚会结束后，我会重新返回列车，继续我的旅行。
t_en: Once this gathering ends, I will return to the Express to continue my journey.

t_ch: 我…还没想明白。
t_en: I... haven't thought it through yet.

t_ch: 我还没想清楚我该如何面对这些事情。但我想会继续旅行。
t_en: I haven't decided how I should confront all this... but I think I will continue my travels.

t_ch: 列车也不可能永载你走下去的，你的朋友们各怀心事，就像曾经的我们一样。
t_en: The Express won't be able to carry you forever. Your friends all have burdens of their own, just as we did back then.

t_ch: 当年逃离罗浮，我曾想过放弃复仇，抛下一切。但随着岁月增长，它越来越清晰，几乎成了与我朝夕相伴的老朋友，时时刻刻在耳畔呢喃。
t_en: I did think about giving up on revenge and letting go of everything when I fled the Luofu years ago. However, as time passed, it became more and more clear... It was like an old friend that accompanied me every passing day, always nagging in my ear.

t_ch: 陷入魔阴身时所作的一切在我的记忆里盘根错节，无法摆脱。最终，我决定面对它。
t_en: The memories of what I did when I was mara-struck are deeply rooted. Since I am unable to rid myself of them, I decided to face them head-on in the end.

t_ch: 当我见证「丰饶」陨落时，也许你和应星都将得到真正的解脱。再见了，饮月。
t_en: Maybe, when I witness the fall of the Abundance, you and Yingxing will find true release. Goodbye, Imbibitor Lunae.

t_ch: 她的剑也做不到，她依旧做不到。
t_en: She couldn't do it with her sword either. She still couldn't do it.

t_ch: 刃冷冷看向你。
t_en: Blade coldly looks at you.

t_ch: 那么我们俩只能继续这场追逐了，这就是你我应付的代价。
t_en: We have no choice but to continue the pursuit. This is the price the two of us must pay.

t_ch: 放弃吧…
t_en: Just give up...

t_ch: 你我都无法彻底结束对方的性命，放弃吧。
t_en: Neither of us can end the other's life. Just give up.

t_ch: 如果你想，我会奉陪到底。
t_en: If you wish, I will see it through.

t_ch: 我会奉陪到底的。
t_en: I will see it through.

t_ch: 在艾利欧的剧本里，你暂时还有活着的必要。
t_en: Elio's script still requires you to stay alive... for now.

t_ch: 而我并不介意将复仇这一过程拉得很漫长，这样也许更有趣。
t_en: I don't mind prolonging my vengeance... It might be more interesting that way.

t_ch: 她走了。
t_en: She's gone.

t_ch: 对新生的你来说，这一切也许看起来像一出拙劣的表演一样。看着老朋友们刀兵相见，我却无法阻止。
t_en: All this must have seemed like a silly performance to someone newly reborn... but I am unable to prevent my old friends from taking sides against one another.

t_ch: 我该为丹枫的作为负责吗？
t_en: Should I be responsible for Dan Feng's actions?

t_ch: 我们是谁这个问题，从来只有我们自己才能回答。
t_en: Who you are is a question that only you can find the answer to...

t_ch: 即便因为龙师的操弄，你从未有过真正的轮回蜕生……
t_en: ...even if the Preceptors' interference denied you a true rebirth...

t_ch: 我要走了。
t_en: I have to go.

t_ch: 我想你也该离开了，丹恒。
t_en: Yes, it has been a long day for you, Dan Heng.

t_ch: 丹恒先生…将军希望我忘掉今天看到的一切。
t_en: Dan Heng... the general wants me to forget about everything I saw today.

t_ch: 他只是想保护你。
t_en: He's just trying to protect you.

t_ch: 我明白，这些纠葛对现在的我来说，还是太复杂了。比剑术要难得多。
t_en: I understand... These disputes are far too complicated for me right now. Swordplay's a lot simpler.

t_ch: 在云骑军里我有不少投契的朋友。假以时日，我们也会这样四分五裂吗？
t_en: I have some close friends of my own in the Cloud Knights... Will we fall out the way you guys did?

t_ch: 我要走了。
t_en: I have to go.

t_ch: 再见，丹恒先生。希望下一次，咱们见面的时候不用这么紧张了。
t_en: Goodbye for now, Dan Heng. I hope the next time we meet, it will be under happier circumstances.

t_ch: 你瞧，星槎已经长成了。
t_en: See? The starskiff is complete.

t_ch: 离开罗浮这么久，我终于能来同你告别了。
t_en: After being away from the Luofu for so long, I can finally say farewell to you.

t_ch: 那位白珩前辈…后来发生了什么？
t_en: So... what happened to Baiheng in the end?

t_ch: 后来啊…应该不用赘述了。今日我们立于此地，便是为了追念她的结局。
t_en: In the end... I do not think the details are necessary. We are here today to commemorate her.

t_ch: 饮月…斩绝「倏忽」那一战，你还记得吗？
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae... Do you still remember the battle against Shuhu?

t_ch: 是与丰饶之民的血战…
t_en: It was a bloody battle against the Denizens of Abundance...

t_ch: 丰饶令使「倏忽」纠集大军，进犯联盟…我在幽囚狱中读过这段史籍。
t_en: The Abundance Emanator, Shuhu, rallied a great army and attacked the Alliance... I read about it in The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 看来你重生后也想寻回自己前世的经历么？
t_en: You seem to be interested in learning about your past life...

t_ch: 只不过这些都被人抹去了。丰饶令使倏忽兵锋犯境，血战后的去向却语焉不详，连他遗骸的下落都不见记载。
t_en: ...still, all of that was erased. After Shuhu attacked the borders, he disappeared without a trace. There are no records of his remains, either.

t_ch: 丹枫也在那片战场上…
t_en: Dan Feng fought in that battle...

t_ch: 丹枫，他也曾和你一同参战？
t_en: Did Dan Feng fight alongside you?

t_ch: 是啊。持明龙尊受人敬仰。得知龙尊亲临战阵，云骑们如履薄冰，当时的腾骁将军便唤我来周护这支部队的安全。
t_en: Yes, the high elder was held in higher regard. The Cloud Knights became wary after learning of Dan Feng's imminent arrival — the General Teng Xiao called for me to guarantee the safety of the troops...

t_ch: 可白珩却不管那些。在她看来，龙尊不过是血肉凡胎，与一旁的士兵无异。她在世的时候，可没少把你差来遣去的。
t_en: ...but Baiheng didn't care about any of that. She believed that the high elder was mortal and no different from the soldier next to her. You ran a fair share of errands for her when she was still around.

t_ch: 我…什么都记不起来。
t_en: I... can't remember any of it.

t_ch: 我什么都记不得了。
t_en: I can't remember anything.

t_ch: 我来告诉你，那一战，白珩这个傻瓜终究还是耗尽了帝弓所赐的运气。
t_en: Let me refresh your memory. During that battle, Baiheng, being the headstrong girl that she was, managed to exhaust the luck that was bestowed upon her by the Reignbow Arbiter.

t_ch: 她只身陷阵，令联盟士卒得以冲破倏忽的「血涂狱界」，更从龙狂中唤回了你。但她却没能走出那片战场，我们都欠下了一笔无法偿还的债。
t_en: She charged ahead and shattered the enemy's defenses, allowing the Xianzhou soldiers to break through Shuhu's Sanguinary Abyss and awaken you from your Dragon's Delirium... She was not able to walk out of that fight. We all shoulder a debt that we will never be able to repay.

t_ch: 对于云骑将士，归葬沙场本是荣耀。可是饮月…你不懂这些。
t_en: For the Cloud Knights, giving up one's life on the battlefield is an honor... but that wasn't something you believed in, Imbibitor Lunae...

t_ch: 你不能接受白珩的离去，竟对她使用了…在她本应该安息的时刻，你犯下了无可挽回的过错。
t_en: You couldn't accept that Baiheng was gone. Instead of leaving her to eternal rest, you decided to... You made a mistake that can never be undone.

t_ch: 不必回应我，答案已经不重要了。饮月，我要送走这艘星槎了。
t_en: There is no need to respond, Imbibitor Lunae, your answer is no longer important... And now, I will send off this starskiff.

t_ch: 真是…熟悉的感觉……
t_en: ...A familiar feeling...

t_ch: …像是回到了你教我剑术的第一日。
t_en: ...almost as if... we've returned to my first sword lesson.

t_ch: 你手执「应星」为你打造的剑器…一遍一遍挑刺、切割、洞穿，一遍又一遍……
t_en: You used the sword Yingxing crafted... and pierced, sliced, and impaled me time and time again...

t_ch: 那些曾经降临在敌人身上的剑招，如今刻在这副可憎的躯壳上，而我只能看着自己的血肉不断抽动、愈合、复原……
t_en: The swordplay used to slay our enemies of old is still engraved on this despicable shell. All I can do is watch my flesh become severed, healed, and restored...

t_ch: 就像在问…「为何，为何要和饮月一起，造下这场恶孽？」
t_en: It's as if they're saying... "Why... why did you and Imbibitor Lunae commit such an unforgivable sin?"

t_ch: 我知道…你不期待我的回答……
t_en: I know that you have no interest in my answer.

t_ch: 所以，当你直视我的眼睛时，我开口提问了。那是我问过自己千百遍，却没有答案的问题——
t_en: ...which is why I asked the question as you looked me straight in the eye. I've asked myself that same question infinite times... but was never able to find the answer...

t_ch: 不一会儿，星槎流水线开始运作了起来…
t_en: Before long, the starskiff assembly line started to operate...

t_ch: 怎么了？{NICKNAME}，找我有事吗？
t_en: What is it, {NICKNAME}? Do you need something?

t_ch: 收到一封给你的信…
t_en: There's a letter for you...

t_ch: 有人邀请你故地重游…
t_en: Someone's inviting you to return to old ground...

t_ch: …没有落款，也没有见面的时间和地点。看来寄信人认为我会记得这些事情。
t_en: ...There's no signature, and it doesn't mention a time or place. Looks like whoever sent this expects me to remember.

t_ch: 很显然，我让他失望了。
t_en: Unfortunately, I'm going to disappoint them.

t_ch: 你的表情很严肃…
t_en: Why so serious?

t_ch: 是么？我只是在思考。
t_en: Huh? I'm just thinking.

t_ch: 需要我帮忙吗？
t_en: Do you need any help?

t_ch: 谢谢，但我自己也还未理清……
t_en: Thanks, but I don't even know where to start...

t_ch: 难道是刃…
t_en: Could it be Blade...?

t_ch: 这封信的字迹行笔确有剑士风范。
t_en: The handwriting does seem like that of a swordmaster, but Blade would've gotten straight to the point.

t_ch: 不过那个男人向来直接了当，「邀请」的方式也锋利无情。这样拐弯抹角的手法，不像他的作风。
t_en: It's an invitation alright, but this is too mysterious to be him.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，把信给我吧，事关丹枫的过去，我必须谨慎对待。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let me hold on to the letter. Anything related to Dan Feng's past should be handled with care.

t_ch: 离开罗浮这么久，这府中的杀气不减反增，倒是令人欣慰。
t_en: After all this time away from the Luofu, the Seat of Divine Foresight has only become more hostile. It's quite reassuring.

t_ch: 喔，说说而已。小弟弟，不必这么如临大敌。我只是在缅怀旧日时光。
t_en: Relax — you needn't be so cautious, young man. I'm just reminiscing about the old days.

t_ch: 不过倒没想到，景元安排的随行之人竟是你。看来你我颇有缘分。
t_en: However, I never thought Jing Yuan would send you to accompany me. Hehe, it would seem our fates are intertwined.

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Hmm?

t_ch: 啊…今天的客人还真是一个接一个……
t_en: I've got my work cut out for me today...

t_ch: 我离开仙舟时，听说他们夺去了你的鳞角，迫使你蜕生，又将你打入幽囚狱中。
t_en: When I left the Xianzhou, I heard they took away your scales and horns, forced you into a hatching rebirth, and detained you in The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 我本以为「饮月君」就此不复存在。但重回罗浮，却再次得见你分海引潮的绝景，真是恍若隔世。
t_en: I thought Imbibitor Lunae had been wiped from this world... but then I returned to the Luofu and watched as you parted the sea. After all these years, it was a magnificent sight to behold.

t_ch: 我…认得你吗？
t_en: Do I... know you?

t_ch: 我们…认识吗？
t_en: Do we... know each other?

t_ch: 嗯…可说认识，也可说不认识。这副淡漠冰冷的口气，倒是和当年那家伙如出一辙。
t_en: Hmm... the answer to that is both yes and no. However, your cold and indifferent tone is identical to the person I knew back then.

t_ch: 我记得你，但不多。
t_en: I remember you, but barely.

t_ch: …我认得你，但记不起更多了。
t_en: ...I recognize you... but I can't remember anything.

t_ch: 前生的经历，对我就像破碎的镜片，拾起哪一枚都只能看见困惑的光景……
t_en: The memories of my past life are like shattered glass... I can't piece together the reality from the individual shards...

t_ch: 「我不记得了」…每个想要逃避过去的人，说辞都如出一辙。
t_en: "I can't remember..." The words of every person who hopes to escape their past.

t_ch: 持明轮回重生，前尘旧事本该一忘成空。可你却留有龙尊的力量，和零星的记忆。
t_en: The Vidyadhara rebirth normally erases all memory of the previous life. However, you have retained fragments of your past, as well as the powers of the high elder.

t_ch: 我的前世已经结束了。
t_en: My past life is over.

t_ch: 你说的没错，「饮月君」的一生已经结束了。现在站在你面前的是另一个人。
t_en: The life of Imbibitor Lunae ended. I stand before you a completely different person.

t_ch: 我明白。持明轮回重生，宿业罪愆也该一笔勾销。如今的你是个游历四方的无名客。不过…人真的能告别过去吗？
t_en: I understand. The sins of a past life should be forgiven after rebirth. Now you are a Nameless, traveling across the galaxy... but can anyone really bid farewell to their past?

t_ch: 若我猜的没错，是龙师们不舍龙脉绝传，想让「饮月君」死灰复燃，故而在蜕鳞之刑上耍了些欺瞒世人的手段，把你变成了这般模样。
t_en: ...If my guess is correct, the Preceptors were unwilling to allow the Ichor Line to end and hoped for Imbibitor Lunae's resurrection. That is why they tampered with the molting rebirth... and turned you into what you are now....

t_ch: 你也想恢复龙尊传承？
t_en: Do you also wish to restore the high elder succession?

t_ch: 所以，你和龙师们一样，也想接续龙尊传承？
t_en: So, you also wish to restore the high elder succession, just like the Preceptors?

t_ch: 这是持明族的私事，与我无关。
t_en: I have no intention of meddling in such things. That is the business of the Vidyadhara.

t_ch: 你想让「饮月君」付出代价？
t_en: You want to make Imbibitor Lunae pay for what they've done?

t_ch: 「饮月君」的所作所为亏欠了许多人。你打算为他们讨回公道？
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae's actions affected many... Are you here to set things straight?

t_ch: 我也不过是一介罪人，有什么资格代天典刑？
t_en: I, too, am a criminal. What right have I to deliver punishment?

t_ch: 说出来意吧。
t_en: Tell me why you're here.

t_ch: 你不是来叙旧的，说出你的来意。
t_en: You're not here to reminisce on old times. Tell me why you're here.

t_ch: 刚才那个持明女孩说得很明白了，怎么，你的尖耳朵不灵了？
t_en: You didn't hear? That Vidyadhara girl explained it clear as day.

t_ch: 我此番回到罗浮，是为了向联盟自首，直面过往的罪愆。
t_en: I returned to the Luofu so I could surrender myself to the Alliance and atone for my sins.

t_ch: 只是在移交受审前，我提出了一个请求。我想宽限一日，会会许久不见的老友们，践行彼此在情深意笃时立下的约定。
t_en: I did, however, have one request before the trial. I asked to be given a single day to go and see old friends and to fulfill promises I made long ago.

t_ch: 景元向来善解人意，又听闻你会赴约，便答应了我的请求。
t_en: Jing Yuan has always been an understanding person. He heard that you would be coming and agreed to my request.

t_ch: 所以，列车上的那封信是你寄出的？
t_en: So, you're the one that mailed the letter to the Express.

t_ch: 与此同时，鳞渊境深处，显龙大雩殿中……
t_en: Meanwhile, in the Dragonvista Rain Hall, Scalegorge Waterscape...

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 与此同时，鳞渊境深处，显龙大雩殿中…
t_en: Meanwhile, in the Dragonvista Rain Hall, Scalegorge Waterscape...

t_ch: 「海若不隐珠，骊龙吐明月」，鳞渊境的风景确如诗中所说，绝美壮丽。
t_en: "The sea's depths conceal no stone, and dragon's breaths reveal the moon." The scenery of Scalegorge Waterscape is truly as magnificent as the poems describe...

t_ch: 只是…我身为嫌犯，理应披枷戴械，在幽囚狱中受审。将军将我带入此地，真的合适吗？
t_en: ...but as a suspect, shouldn't I be bound and interrogated in The Shackling Prison? Is it really appropriate to bring me to a place like this, General?

t_ch: 幽囚狱中关押的麻烦太多，不宜再添一桩。安全起见，请你在这受审吧。
t_en: Holding you in The Shackling Prison would be more trouble than it's worth. For security purposes, we will be performing your trial here.

t_ch: 安全起见…看来不是为我的安全啊。
t_en: For security purposes... I suppose it's not my security you're talking about.

t_ch: 星核降临，建木苏生，天舶司的接渡使遭人偷梁换柱，真身则是毁灭的令使。
t_en: The Stellaron's descent, the Ambrosial Arbor's resurrection, the Destruction Emanator's impersonation of the Amicassador...

t_ch: 依照天舶司的追查，你随商队来到罗浮，停驻数日，却未与任何人有贸易往来。本该离去之日，又恰逢孽灾爆发。
t_en: According to the Sky-Faring Commission's investigations, you arrived on the Luofu with a group of merchants, but didn't make a single trade during your stay. Your departure coincided with the day the calamities took place.

t_ch: 你趁乱潜入幽囚狱，却也无所作为。如今又宣称自己要为这场星核灾变负责，伏罪自首。
t_en: You took advantage of the chaos to sneak into The Shackling Prison, but seemingly took no course of action. Now, you claim that you are responsible for the Stellaron crisis and wish to surrender yourself.

t_ch: 奇怪，奇怪，阁下的行为可真令人捉摸不透。
t_en: Your behavior truly is puzzling... I can't seem to piece together your motive.

t_ch: 我身为行商，受人所托运送「信物」，并不知晓背后隐情。踏入幽囚狱确有所图，但现在看来，罗浮并没有我索求之物。
t_en: I was asked to deliver a token in my capacity as a traveling merchant — I knew nothing of its significance. I admit, I did have my own agenda for entering The Shackling Prison... but in the end, I found that the Luofu did not possess what I was looking for.

t_ch: 而认罪伏法，则是畏惧惩罚。银河虽大，我区区一介行商，想必逃不过整个联盟的追捕。
t_en: It is through fear of punishment that I am choosing to surrender. The galaxy is a vast place, but I am merely a wanderer. It would be silly of me to think I could outrun the Alliance.

t_ch: 区区一介行商？罗刹先生说笑了。你在哀荣堡所行之事，在面纱星域遗留的种种过往，需要我一一细述吗？
t_en: Merely a wanderer? You're too modest, Luocha. Do I need to remind you of your actions at the Eternity Fortress and the Shroudveil Starzone...?

t_ch: 还是说，你需要我道出那个拗口的名字？
t_en: ...or must I utter that tongue-twister of a name?

t_ch: …哦，「神策将军」果然名不虚传。
t_en: I... Well, they don't call you the Divine Foresight for nothing, General.

t_ch: 彼此彼此，所幸我预先做了些功课，不然今日这场对谈可要冷场了。
t_en: You flatter me. Fortunately, I did my research, or this conversation would've been quite dry.

t_ch: 既然将军觉得事有蹊跷，那是要替我辩护，洗脱「星核嫌犯」的疑罪？
t_en: Seeing that you sense something is amiss, are you looking to defend me and clear my name of suspicion?

t_ch: 此事由不得我。事关倾覆联盟的重罪，依照法度，你当被押入虚陵仙舟，接受十王司和七天将的联席审判，并施以永罚。
t_en: That is beyond my control. Attempting to undermine the Alliance is a grave crime. According to protocol, you are to be held on the Xianzhou Xuling for a joint trial before the Ten-Lords Commission and the Seven Arbiter-Generals.

t_ch: 不过嘛…眼下这一时片刻，阁下还有机会欣赏鳞渊境的美景，这将是你最后能见到的罗浮景象。
t_en: However... at present you have the opportunity to enjoy the beautiful scenery of Scalegorge Waterscape. It will be the last view of the Luofu you ever lay eyes on.

t_ch: 我听她说过，数百年前，「云上五骁」曾在这儿相聚宴饮。
t_en: She told me that the High-Cloud Quintet once gathered and feasted here hundreds of years ago.

t_ch: 令师镜流的绝世剑技，饮月君丹枫的云吟奇术，飞行士白珩驾驭星槎的巧技，辅以朱明巧匠应星所锻造的神兵利器，还有将军的智策运筹……
t_en: The unparalleled sword techniques of your master, the mesmerizing cloudhymn magic of "Imbibitor Lunae" Dan Feng, the artful piloting skills of Baiheng, the divine weapons crafted by the Zhuming artisan Yingxing, and the unmatched stratagem of the General...

t_ch: 五位追魔扫秽，留下诸多令人神往的传奇。可惜，事无长久。最终「云上五骁」还是四分五裂，彼此陌路。
t_en: The five of you vanquished evil from the world, creating legendary tales in your wake. Unfortunately, nothing lasts forever, and the High-Cloud Quintet disbanded as each of you went your separate ways.

t_ch: 将军将我带离幽囚狱，来此问话，显然不是为了那里的安全，而是想单独谈些避人耳目的话题。
t_en: You removed me from The Shackling Prison and decided to interrogate me here... but not for security purposes. You wish to discuss things that shouldn't be overheard.

t_ch: 关于你问讯至今，却始终避而不谈的那个人…令师镜流。
t_en: You wish to discuss the one person you've avoided mentioning up to now... your master — Jingliu.

t_ch: 大姐姐，你该不会是故意放水，让我先到的吧？
t_en: You didn't go easy just so I would get here first, did you?

t_ch: 怎么会，是你剑术精进神速，我赶不上了。
t_en: Of course not. Your technique must have improved so much that I simply couldn't keep up with you.

t_ch: 那这一次，咱们是要祭拜那位短生种前辈吗？
t_en: Hmm... So are we here to commemorate your short-life species friend?

t_ch: 偷听别人说话可不好唷。何况，我几时说过他已不在人世了？
t_en: Eavesdropping is a bad habit. Besides, when did I ever say that he was no longer in this world?

t_ch: 以短生种的寿数，便是躲进休眠舱里也拖延不了太久。就算他还活着，怕如今也是个垂垂老人了。
t_en: Hmph, even the cryo capsules wouldn't be able to extend the life expectancy of a short-life species for long. If he is still alive, he must be ancient.

t_ch: 他还活着？
t_en: He's still alive?

t_ch: 应星…还活着？
t_en: Yingxing is... still alive?

t_ch: 他当然还活着，但也许还不如死了。
t_en: Of course he's still alive... but he might be better off dead.

t_ch: 他成为了长生种？
t_en: He became a long-life species?

t_ch: 他转变成了长生种？
t_en: He transformed into a long-life species?

t_ch: 猜的不错。命运就是这般爱开玩笑。
t_en: Correct. Such are the twists of destiny.

t_ch: 有些人纵然天慧耀眼、智光昭昭，却总在命运转折时，做出最愚笨的选择。
t_en: Some people are born with unparalleled foresight and exceptional intelligence but make the most ill-advised choices at destiny's crossroads.

t_ch: 聪明如他，竟妄想用那位丰饶令使的血肉，帮助饮月将阵亡入灭的挚友带回人世。
t_en: As smart as he was, he made the foolish attempt to use the flesh of the Abundance Emanator to aid Imbibitor Lunae in resurrecting his deceased companion.

t_ch: 他的愚行最终把自己变作了不死的怪物，魂消魄陨，堕为生前最鄙夷的恶孽——真是造化弄人啊……
t_en: His stupidity turned him into a monster, cursed with immortality. His soul was shattered and became the evil that he hated most. The irony...

t_ch: 你好像对这个老朋友毫无同情的意思啊。
t_en: For an old friend, you don't seem to have that much pity...

t_ch: 我给了他重来一次的机会，这还算不上同情吗？
t_en: I gave him a second chance — does that not count?

t_ch: 重来一次？
t_en: A second chance?

t_ch: 你对他做了什么？
t_en: What did you do to him?

t_ch: 以他的所作所为，本该永镇幽囚狱中。可我给了他另一种「自由」。
t_en: He should have been eternally jailed in The Shackling Prison for his crimes, but I gave him a different kind of "freedom."

t_ch: 我带走那具已成空壳的形骸，授他剑法，赐他百死，教他永远不忘前世业报。听说他重获新生后，还为自己取了个名字…
t_en: I took the shell that once held his soul and passed my sword techniques on to him. I let him die a hundred deaths... to remind him of the karma of his previous life. Afterward, I heard he was reborn and even gave himself a name...

t_ch: 时间消磨得差不多了，接下来，我们去丹鼎司吧。
t_en: We've idled too long. Let's head to the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 与此同时，鳞渊境深处，显龙大雩殿中…
t_en: Meanwhile, in the Dragonvista Rain Hall, Scalegorge Waterscape...

t_ch: …身为帝弓的追随者，将军必然见识过寰宇诸界遭受寿瘟荼毒的惨状。那些生灵或化作不死的魔物，或沦为献祭丰饶的羔羊。
t_en: As a follower of the Reignbow Arbiter, you have witnessed the catastrophe brought upon by the Plagues Author, General. Living beings turning into undying monsters, eventually used as sacrifices to the Abundance.

t_ch: 将军认为，该如何平息这一浩劫？
t_en: What do you think should be done to put an end to it, General?

t_ch: 联盟奉帝弓诰谕，除魔不止，为的正是有朝一日能铲除药师，令生死重回正轨。
t_en: The Alliance follows the Reignbow Arbiter's edict — to continue ridding the world of evil, so that one day Yaoshi might be vanquished and the cycle of life and death restored.

t_ch: 帝弓巡猎，云骑景从，不计牺牲讨魔守正，确实令人敬佩，可惜…却不免狭隘。
t_en: Under the auspices of the Reignbow Arbiter, the faithful Cloud Knights continue The Hunt. Their devotion and sacrifices are indeed commendable... but their devotedness has made them close-minded.

t_ch: 正如我的力量来自「丰饶」，立场却与联盟一致——宇宙间要置药师于死地的，并非只有「巡猎」一方势力。
t_en: Take me, for example. My powers originate from the Abundance, yet my goals are aligned with the Alliance. The Hunt is not the only faction in the universe that wants Yaoshi dead.

t_ch: 苦于短生的顽疾，向往永生的良药，这是智慧生灵的常情。要断绝这些念想，就像要杀死一位星神，荒诞不堪，几近笑谈。
t_en: It is natural for short-life-species to yearn for immortality — that is expected of any intelligent life form. To want to eliminate these thoughts would be equivalent to killing an Aeon. Such a goal is so impossible as to be laughable, to be honest.

t_ch: 所以，要彻底斩断药师的诅咒，便得从根源上另寻他法。
t_en: So, in order to sever Yaoshi's curse, one must look to the source for a different solution.

t_ch: 令师得蒙天启，从魔阴中归来，又行遍诸界，已找到了解开这一死结的方法……
t_en: Your master happened upon a revelation and returned from the mara. She has traveled many worlds and discovered a solution...

t_ch: 我听着。
t_en: I'm listening.

t_ch: 抱歉，将军。看来下一步棋…要在「虚陵」落子了。
t_en: I apologize, General. I'm afraid the next move... will have to be made on the Xuling.

t_ch: 果真如我所料，认罪伏法只是手段。利用「十王敕令」前往虚陵，直面六将军乃至元帅…这才是你们真正的目的。
t_en: Hmph... as I expected. Your surrender is just the next step in your scheme. You wish to avail yourself of the Ten Lords' decree to enter the Xuling and stand before the six Generals — even the Marshal. That is your true agenda...

t_ch: ——而那「棺中之物」，正是为此准备的吧。
t_en: ...and the purpose of the contents of your coffin.

t_ch: 将军，方才你也说过，此事由不得你我。联盟法度如此，不容更改。
t_en: General, you said it yourself — this is beyond your control. It is the law of the Alliance, we cannot deviate from it.

t_ch: 漂亮，漂亮，这步棋，下得精彩。
t_en: Splendid, well done. This was a spectacular move.

t_ch: 你真的变了，景元。
t_en: You've changed, Jing Yuan.

t_ch: 如今的你，竟会承认自己棋差一着。
t_en: The man I knew would never have admitted he was bested.

t_ch: 丹鼎司已恢复了秩序，你来这儿干嘛？
t_en: Order's already been restored to the Alchemy Commission... Why are we here?

t_ch: 我听说「衔药龙女」能医百病，也想像普通人一样，求医问药，看个门诊。
t_en: I heard the Healer Lady is capable of curing all diseases. I'm here for the same reason anyone would be — for a check-up and consultation.

t_ch: 不必心急，这里离鳞渊境不过一程之遥，你家将军交代的任务，快要结束了。
t_en: Patience, young man — we're not far from Scalegorge Waterscape. The mission the general tasked you with is about to come to an end.

t_ch: 将这个男人先带走吧，接下来的事情与他无关。
t_en: Take this man away. This does not involve him.

t_ch: 彦卿。
t_en: Yanqing.

t_ch: …这样，人便到齐了。没想到阔别数百年后，「云上五骁」还能再度聚首。
t_en: ...And so, everyone is present. I never thought the High-Cloud Quintet would be able to gather again in the same place after hundreds of years.

t_ch: 如果我所记不差，七百年前，我们五人便是在这儿立下承诺，无论间关迢迢，都要相聚在此共饮一杯。
t_en: If I remember correctly, the five of us made a promise here seven centuries ago. We promised that no matter what happened, we would gather here together and share a drink.

t_ch: 可惜鳞渊空悬，世事蓬转。我们五人有的在世重生，有的求死不能，有的人沦为罪囚，而有的人…再也没法赴约了。彼此情谊也荡然无存。
t_en: How sad that Scalegorge Waterscape remains empty while the world continues to turn. Some of us have been reborn, while others were denied death. Some have become criminals, while others... can no longer fulfill their promises. And in the end, our friendship is no more.

t_ch: 很快我将负枷受审，此去一别，也许是永别。所以我要在离开之前发出邀请，邀请各位在这初聚之地道别。
t_en: Soon, I will be shackled and tried. This will be the last you see of me. This is why I sent out the invitations before departing, hoping that everyone would be gathered here for my final farewell.

t_ch: 「人有五名，代价有三。」
t_en: Of five people, three must pay a price.

t_ch: 祸首饮月，一意孤行，擅行化龙妙法起死回生，变化形骸，酿致大祸，有辱战士哀荣。
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae, the chief culprit, chose to use the Transmutation Arcanum as a means of resurrection, begetting terrible transformation and great calamity in the process. He dishonored the name of the fallen.

t_ch: 从凶应星，狂悖骄慢，染指丰饶神使血肉，助饮月妄为，终至堕为不死孽物。
t_en: Yingxing, the accomplice, arrogant and oblivious, used the flesh of the Abundance Emanator to assist Imbibitor Lunae in his evil. He fell, becoming an immortal abomination.

t_ch: …而罪人镜流，身犯魔阴，弑杀同袍，背弃盟谊。
t_en: As for Jingliu, the sinner... She succumbed to mara, slaughtered her people, and abandoned her oath.

t_ch: 现在，该是我们付出代价的时候了。
t_en: Now, it is time we pay for what we have done.
